page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96 is a general-purpose server powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs derived from 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 360 GB of RAM, providing 3.75 GB of memory per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for matrix operations. This instance does not include local storage or bundled public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark testing, the c4-standard-96 demonstrates top-tier multi-threaded capabilities. It achieves a PassMark CPU score of 83,403.58 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 21,415.0, both placing in the top 10% of tested servers. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches 538.12 GB/sec, and static web serving throughput is recorded at 126.92 GB/sec. Single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance falling into the average category at 2103.98. Memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 288.83 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. The hardware-assisted matrix extensions contribute to strong prompt processing speeds in LLM inference, reaching 289.79 tokens/sec on medium 7B models.

From a resource density perspective, the c4-standard-96 offers high compute density with a balanced memory allocation, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for highly parallelized workloads. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy tasks. The server is highly suitable for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference prompt processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96 is a general-purpose server configured with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 360 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz, it features 48 physical cores and a 260 MB L3 cache. The architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 83,403.58 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 21,415.0, alongside strong memory read bandwidth of 288.83 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is average, and the instance lacks local storage and bundled public IPv4 addresses. This server is optimized for highly parallelized workloads, including software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.

tagline: A high-density, multi-core x86_64 server featuring Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs and 360 GB of RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor
Top-tier multi-core performance with a PassMark CPU score of 83,403.58
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support accelerating matrix and vector operations
No local storage or bundled public IPv4 addresses included
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving
